title: Rumors from the South
text: Merchants speak of a great mortality in ports across the sea. Some say it travels with ships and fleas; others say it is God’s scourge.

choices.label: Order prayers and processions.
Inspect ports and restrict arrivals.
Dismiss it as foreign panic.

title: The Pestilence Arrives
text: Buboes and fever appear in a coastal town. Within days, households are struck down. Fear spreads faster than any rider.

choices.label: Fund hospitals and burial crews.
Let towns manage it; keep coin for war and law.

title: Labor Grows Scarce
text: So many are dead that fields go unploughed. Survivors demand higher wages and freer movement; lords complain their customary dues are failing.

choices.label: Freeze wages by decree.
Allow bargaining in towns and manors.
Bind workers to their villages by ordinance.

title: An Empty Manor
text: A steward reports that half a village is gone. The remaining tenants cannot meet the old dues. The manor court waits for your decision.

choices.label: Reduce dues to keep tenants on the land.
Demand the customary dues in full.
Commute labor dues into cash payments.

title: The Realm Debates Wage Law
text: Your council proposes strict enforcement against ‘excess wages’ and wandering servants. Sheriffs ask for clear orders.

choices.label: Enforce wage law harshly.
Enforce it lightly; avoid provoking the commons.

title: Whispers of Heresy
text: Some priests preach that the pestilence proves the Church is corrupt. Others blame foreigners or Jews. You must keep order, and truth.

choices.label: Back the bishops and punish agitators.
Hold a royal inquiry; calm the town with justice.
Ignore it; you have bigger problems.

title: The Wool Merchants Petition
text: Merchants ask for safer roads and predictable tolls. In return they offer loans, at interest the clergy dislike.

choices.label: Grant charters and protect their trade.
Squeeze them with new tolls.

title: Royal Justices on Circuit
text: Your justices report local lords overriding customary courts. The commons ask for the king’s peace.

choices.label: Enforce royal law over local courts.
Let local lords handle it in their own courts.

title: A Call for Levies
text: Your captains ask for men and horses. Lords offer service, but complain the realm cannot spare labor after the mortality.

choices.label: Demand feudal service as owed.
Hire paid soldiers instead.

title: A Poor Harvest
text: Rain and blight ruin grain. Granaries thin and tempers rise; your officers warn of unrest if bread prices climb.

choices.label: Release grain stores and cap prices.
Let the market set the price.

title: Grumbling Over Taxes
text: Collectors report resistance. Villages say they have buried too many dead to pay as before.

choices.label: Send sheriffs with strict orders.
Offer relief in the worst-hit shires.

title: Sparks in the Southeast
text: Rumors spread of villages refusing dues and officials being driven off. The air smells of pitch and anger.

choices.label: Meet them and promise reforms.
Crush the rebels with force.

title: After the Rising
text: The revolt breaks, but memory remains. Lords demand renewed control; towns fear further disorder.

choices.label: Confirm harsh penalties and strengthen manorial courts.
Quietly ease dues to prevent another spark.

title: A Marriage Alliance
text: A great family offers a marriage tie. They want offices and influence in return.

choices.label: Accept; bind them to the crown.
Refuse; keep court factions weak.

title: More Scribes, More Writs
text: Your chancellor proposes expanding royal administration. More clerks would mean more orders obeyed, if you can pay them.

choices.label: Expand the royal bureaucracy.
Rely on lords and custom.

title: A New Wave of Sickness
text: After years of uneasy calm, sickness returns in pockets. Some flee to the countryside; others barricade streets.

choices.label: Reinforce burial crews and isolate the sick.
Do nothing; let it burn out.

title: The Pestilence Eases
text: Cases fall. Survivors speak of new freedoms, and landlords speak of lost dues. The realm breathes again, unevenly.

choices.label: Hold thanksgiving and rebuild quietly.
Use the lull to tighten labor laws.

title: Bandits on the Roads
text: Travelers are robbed; a sheriff asks funds for patrols. Merchants complain trade is slowing.

choices.label: Pay for patrols and hang the worst offenders.
Leave it to local lords.

title: An Overseas Venture
text: A merchant consortium offers you a stake in a ship bound for distant ports. If it returns, the profit will be handsome. If it is lost, the court will whisper that you gambled the realm’s coin.

choices.label: Invest in the ship.
Refuse. The Crown does not gamble.
